data.title: Collapse of Crypto-Backed Stocks: Market-to-NAV ratios plummet, some trading below 1.0 value of Bitcoin or Ethereum held. Dilution, panic selling fueling death spiral in financial illusion. Gap widens between asset value and market perception. Company's balance sheet strengthens while equity valuation weakens. Retail investors facing declines while BTC soars. Grim options for firms as unsustainable models shatter.
data.description: The article discusses the collapse of the digital asset treasury company (DATC) model, highlighting the structural precariousness of the current situation. Market-to-Net Asset Value (mNAV) ratios are plummeting, with companies like Strategy trading at a steep discount from their previous premiums. Some companies, like Bitmine Immersion and SharpLink, have seen their mNAV dip below 1.0. The core issue is nonstop dilution, as these firms rely on At-The-Market (ATM) equity programs to raise capital, leading to a self-cannibalizing cycle. Retail investors are losing confidence as their holdings lag behind Bitcoin's performance. The article emphasizes the need for fundamental changes to address the widening gap between asset value and market perception before the situation worsens.

data.title: OpenAI halts Martin Luther King Jr. videos due to racist content created by users on its Sora app.
data.description: The Sora video app has been used by individuals to create offensive and occasionally racist portrayals of the civil rights leader.

data.title: Wolters Kluwer presents UpToDate Expert AI at HLTH 2025, empowered with clinical AI to streamline decision-making.
data.description: Wolters Kluwer Health, based in Waltham, Massachusetts, is set to exhibit the advancements in clinical decision support through Expert AI at HLTH 2025, specifically at booth #3218. The focus will be on showcasing the capabilities of the newly introduced UpToDate Expert AI. This AI tool is tailored to provide healthcare institutions with reliable, evidence-driven AI assistance. By integrating UpToDate Expert AI, healthcare providers can enhance clinical processes and expedite well-informed decision-making using enhanced clinical insights. Alongside this, Wolters Kluwer Health will introduce other cutting-edge solutions at the event.

data.title: Mt. Gox's specter to vanish from Bitcoin by Halloween after a tumultuous journey. From Tokyo Whale to the deadline, the saga concludes.
data.description: As Mt. Gox's saga in Bitcoin history draws to a close, from the Tokyo Whale to the upcoming Halloween deadline, significant developments mark its journey. The Tokyo Whale, known for selling large amounts of Bitcoin, played a crucial role in Mt. Gox's story. Now, as the Halloween deadline approaches, final legal proceedings are underway to address the fallout from Mt. Gox's collapse, involving the distribution of remaining assets worth millions of dollars to creditors. This marks a significant milestone in the resolution of one of the most infamous incidents in cryptocurrency history.

data.title: Global AI access democratized with decentralized compute networks freeing up idle GPUs, centralized AI compute in developed nations
data.description: The distribution of AI compute power continues to be concentrated in developed countries, with a significant portion of it centralized. However, decentralized blockchain networks have the potential to change this by utilizing unused GPUs and making access to AI technologies more widespread. This shift could lead to a more democratized distribution of computational resources, improving access to AI technologies for individuals worldwide.

data.title: Charles Schwab Corp. surpasses Q3 expectations with adjusted EPS of $1.31 (+70% Y/Y) and sales of $6.135 billion (+27% Y/Y). President & CEO Rick Wurster credits organic growth trends, wealth solutions, and favorable macroeconomic conditions for the success. Client transactional sweep cash grew by $13.5 billion.
data.description: The Charles Schwab Corporation (NYSE: SCHW) announced strong third-quarter results, surpassing expectations. The company posted adjusted earnings per share of $1.31, marking a 70% increase from the previous year and exceeding the anticipated $1.25. Quarterly sales reached $6.135 billion, reflecting a 27% year-over-year growth, higher than the estimated $6.009 billion. President & CEO Rick Wurster highlighted the record revenue and earnings per share driven by improving organic growth trends, rising adoption of wealth solutions, and favorable macroeconomic conditions. Client transactional sweep cash saw a significant increase of $13.5 billion compared to the previous quarter.

data.title: Global battery materials market projected to grow from $83.95 billion in 2025 to $147.93 billion by 2030 with a CAGR of 13.7%.
data.description: Dublin, - The latest report on the battery materials market offers insights into various battery types such as Lead-Acid and Lithium-Ion, along with materials like Cathode (LFP, LCO, NMC, NCA, LMO), Anode, and Electrolyte. This report covers applications in sectors including Automotive, Electric Vehicles, Portable Devices, and Industrial usage. The global market for battery materials is expected to witness significant growth, with projections indicating an increase from USD 83.95 billion in 2025 to USD 147.93 billion by 2030. This growth signifies a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 13.7%. The detailed analysis aims to provide valuable market insights and forecasts for stakeholders.

data.title: Seven Seas Water Group starts project for a SWRO desalination plant in Aruba with Water-as-a-Service®.
data.description: ORANJESTAD, Aruba-- Water- en Energiebedrijf Aruba N.V. (“WEB”) and Seven Seas Water Group have initiated the construction of a cutting-edge seawater reverse osmosis (SWRO) desalination plant in Aruba. This project, awarded to Seven Seas Aruba affiliate in 2024, signifies a significant step in the country’s infrastructure advancement. The plant aims to address the growing water demand by producing a considerable amount of treated water upon completion. The joint effort between WEB and Seven Seas demonstrates a commitment to enhancing Aruba's water supply capabilities.

data.title: U.S. Bancorp beats Q3 earnings estimates with adjusted EPS of $1.22, net income of $2.001 billion, and $7.30 billion in sales. CEO cites growth and expense management for increase.
data.description: U.S. Bancorp (NYSE: USB) reported strong financial results for the third quarter, surpassing expectations. Their adjusted earnings per share stood at $1.22, exceeding the projected $1.13. Quarterly sales reached $7.30 billion, higher than the anticipated $7.16 billion. The bank recorded a net income of $2.001 billion, marking a 16.7% increase from the previous year. CEO Gunjan Kedia attributed this success to net interest income growth, margin expansion, and robust performance in fee businesses, backed by efficient expense management. The company is optimistic about the fourth quarter.

data.title: UK colocation market analysis reports VIRTUS Data Centres as top operator; London leads data center market with over 1.7 GW of capacity.
data.description: In Dublin, the ResearchAndMarkets.com database has included the "United Kingdom Existing & Upcoming Data Center Portfolio." VIRTUS Data Centres holds the top position in the UK, with Equinix and Digital Realty following closely behind. The current data center capacity across the UK stands at approximately 1.7 gigawatts, accompanied by over 10 million square feet of developed space. London emerges as the frontrunner in the upcoming data center market within the UK, contributing more than 60% of the total power capacity. The demand for cloud and tech services in the region is steadily rising.
